canonical_wording: Simple contamination is nearly perfectly localizable on average (AUPRC ~0.93), whereas structured contamination is markedly less detectable on average (AUPRC ~0.35) but highly heterogeneous. Temporal M04/M05 remain difficult (~0.13-0.14); entity/source M08/M09 are moderately detectable (~0.43-0.69).
allowed_short: Structured contamination is heterogeneous in detectability, not uniformly hard.
prohibited: Structured contamination is statistically undetectable / AUPRC 0.05-0.07 / all structured hard to localize.

canonical_wording: Detectability and exploitability exhibit a positive overall association across 11 mechanisms (Pearson r ~0.69), with wide uncertainty (n=11). Category explains substantial variance but detectability retains incremental value (dR2 ~0.11). Strong within simple and structured; boundary is exception (M03 influential).
allowed_short: Detectability and exploitability are positively associated; category is not the sole driver.

canonical_wording: Model family is associated with exploitation magnitude (boosting trees largest, LR smallest, TabM intermediate), but differences are modest (~0.02-0.04 AUROC; ratios ~1.1-1.3x), not 2.2-2.5x.
allowed_short: Model family measurably but modestly affects exploitation.
prohibited: RF 2.2x / CatBoost 2.5x / capacity causally drives exploitation.

canonical_wording: Three-axis mechanism profiles are broadly consistent across five model families (mean pairwise Spearman ~0.85, Kendall W ~0.88), not universal: TabM slightly negative on M04/M05; disagreement near M02/M03.
allowed_short: Profiles broadly consistent with mechanism-specific exceptions.
prohibited: Fully consistent / invariant across all models.
